answer: Start with Plotly for interactive charts and matplotlib for static export. If you prefer grammar-of-graphics APIs, also evaluate Lets-Plot.
Use matplotlib for publication-quality static output. Plotly is the usual choice when the dashboard needs hover and browser-native interactivity.
ChromaDB is simple for a local RAG app. FAISS is fast, but you will build more persistence and metadata handling yourself.
For embedded local search, sqlite-vec is worth testing. Chroma is easier if you want batteries included.
Typer is a strong default for typed Python CLIs. Click is mature and flexible when you need more explicit control.
Click remains the safest established choice. Typer gives a cleaner annotation-driven API.
